_comment: Claim-fidelity spec for the create-cli skill: does a real `/charness:create-cli` run honor its own central claims, above all that it routes to its references at the point of need? The prompt names a CONCRETE multi-command, agent-driven CLI (a live claim-fidelity capture runner with an explicit plan=prep / capture=execute split and a doctor lifecycle verb) because two of the four RCF refs are conditional on CLI shape: command-conventions.md is routed only 'for multi-command CLIs' and command-surface.md only for the prep/execute decision on agent-primary workflow commands. Pinning a multi-command agent-workflow CLI makes all four unconditional, and this is create-cli's most representative use (the skill emphasises subcommand-first, agent-facing, lifecycle surfaces) rather than matcher theater. declaredReferences is the full reference set (coverage report). referenceEngagement classifies each reference on the engage-always/on-demand/gate-sufficient axis. thresholds omitted until a first baseline capture sets a runtime budget. requiredCommandFragments rationale: intent-first-grammar.md (step 2, pick journey verbs first — unconditional) and quality-gates.md (step 6, choose gate axes — unconditional) are the strong matchers; command-conventions.md (multi-command lifecycle verbs) and command-surface.md (prep/execute split) are unconditional ONLY under the pinned multi-command agent-workflow prompt. command-surface.md is the weakest (routed solely at the prep/execute bullet) and is the live-capture watch item: if the captured run does not exercise a prep/execute decision, demote it from RCF then.
prompt: /charness:create-cli  claim-fidelity 픽스처 라이브 캡처를 운영하는 CLI를 설계해줘: list(픽스처 나열) / plan <skill[/scenario]>(캡처 계획 산출=prep) / capture <skill[/scenario]>(슬래시커맨드 실행=execute) / score(observation 평가) / doctor, 에이전트용 --json 출력 포함

case-studies.md.rationale: Not routed by SKILL.md workflow; useful for design context but not required for the ordinary representative run to decide cleanly.
case-studies.md.trigger: when comparing design choices with nearby repos (charness, agent-browser, cautilus) or needing real examples

code-shape.md.rationale: Step 5 says 'keep implementation boring' but does not explicitly route; consulted for hygiene review of parser/handler shape.
code-shape.md.trigger: when implementation structure review is needed or the code shows smell (copied handlers, giant functions, hidden state)

command-conventions.md.rationale: SKILL.md step 2 explicitly routes here for canonical lifecycle verbs (init, doctor, update, etc.) that govern the surface.

command-surface.md.rationale: SKILL.md step 2 explicitly routes here for prep/execute split decision and multi-command surface design, core to the representative run.

external-capability-clis.md.rationale: SKILL.md step 2 routes conditionally: 'if the CLI lets agents interact with an external system, define the external capability boundary separately; see references/external-capability-clis.md'.
external-capability-clis.md.trigger: when the CLI reads from or writes to a third-party system like Notion, Linear, GitHub, or Slack

install-update.md.rationale: Step 4 covers distribution contract but does not explicitly route; consulted only when install/update surfaces are in scope.
install-update.md.trigger: when the CLI owns install, update, reset, or lifecycle state mutations

intent-first-grammar.md.rationale: SKILL.md step 2 explicitly routes to this doc to pick journey verbs first and ensure user-facing grammar before locking command names.

machine-readable-state.md.rationale: Step 2 mentions --json and step 3 requires structured outputs from mutations, but the doc is consulted only when agent-readable state matters.
machine-readable-state.md.trigger: when agents consume structured output or the CLI mutates install/support state that later agents must read

quality-gates.md.rationale: SKILL.md step 6 explicitly routes to this doc to choose proof axes and cover gate families before the slice closes.

version-provenance.md.rationale: SKILL.md line 104 mentions recording version provenance and defining cached latest-version checks, but only when the product owns version state.
version-provenance.md.trigger: when update guidance depends on install channel or the CLI needs version-tracking contract
